search_objective: Locate system-relevant implementation artifacts for the Gov_Reg ID / registry / remediation system

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Explicitly the ONLY module allowed for import for ID allocation per its own docstring. Wraps UnifiedIDAllocator. Provides allocate_id, allocate_file_id, allocate_enhanced_id, allocate_dir_id, validate_id.
risks: Loads default facade lazily via module global; concurrent initializations not guarded at facade layer

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Backend allocator. Generates 20-digit IDs: BASE_ID_PREFIX (01260207201, 11 digits) + 9-digit counter. Uses atomic_json_write for counter persistence. Marked INTERNAL by facade contract.
risks: Imports shared_utils from a hardcoded relative path '01260207201000001276_scripts' which may fail if instantiated from a different working directory
Comment says 22-digit but code produces 20-digit; minor docstring inconsistency

best_candidates.relevance_reason: GEU (Governance Entity Unit) ID generation scheme using CRC32-based anchor hashing. Separate from primary 20-digit allocator.
risks: Produces IDs with prefix '99' which does not match the standard 01260207201 prefix schema

conflicts_or_gaps: add_ids_recursive.py (cli tool) directly imports UnifiedIDAllocator bypassing the facade, violating the stated contract
P_01999000042260124489_id_scheme.py uses a completely different ID format (CRC32-based GEU IDs vs counter-based 20-digit IDs)

recommended_authority.reason: Explicitly declared as the ONLY allowed allocation entrypoint; enforced by pre-commit gate

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Full reservation lifecycle: RESERVED → COMMITTED → CANCELLED → EXPIRED state machine. Per-plan ledger files stored as RES-{plan_id}.json. Atomic reads/writes.
risks: imports shared_utils from 'parent/scripts' relative path that may resolve differently from allocators directory

conflicts_or_gaps: No caller of ReservationLedger found within the main REGISTRY runtime; IdpkgEngine does not appear to use it directly

recommended_authority.reason: Only reservation ledger implementation found; fully self-contained

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Provides atomic_json_write (lock file + temp file + atomic rename), atomic_json_read, acquire_file_lock, release_file_lock with Windows (msvcrt) and Unix (fcntl) support. Used by UnifiedIDAllocator, ReservationLedger, and IdpkgRuntime.
risks: Located in 01260207201000001276_scripts outside REGISTRY/ID tree; path resolution is fragile (each importer uses a different relative path to find it)

conflicts_or_gaps: DirIdManager.write_dir_id in dir_id_handler.py implements its own independent atomic write via temp file + replace without using shared_utils

recommended_authority.reason: Only canonical atomic write implementation; used by counter store and reservation ledger

best_candidates.relevance_reason: watchdog-based DirIdWatcher (FileSystemEventHandler subclass) for .dir_id enforcement on directory create/delete/modify. Emits JSON evidence. Also exports watch_governed_zones() CLI entry.
risks: Delegates to ZoneClassifier that imports from P_01260207233100000068_zone_classifier but the file under handlers imports P_01260207233100000069_dir_id_handler; the filename mismatch indicates a possible import confusion

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Alternative WatcherDaemon implementation using watchdog + WorkQueue + SuppressionManager from repo_autoops module. Different architecture from dir_id_watcher.
risks: Imports from repo_autoops.automation_descriptor which was NOT found on disk in the REGISTRY tree - likely a broken import
Enqueues to WorkQueue but no consumer of that queue was found

conflicts_or_gaps: Two separate watcher implementations with incompatible designs; watcher_daemon.py depends on repo_autoops module not present

recommended_authority.reason: Self-contained, uses standard watchdog, integrates with live DirectoryIdentityResolver

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Implements S10D-S12D pipeline: detect anchor, validate anchor, allocate dir_id if missing in governed zone. Returns IdentityResolutionResult dataclass. Calls allocate_dir_id from facade.
risks: Imports P_01260207233100000069_dir_id_handler but the main handler file is P_01999000042260125068_dir_id_handler.py - two different handler implementations may exist

conflicts_or_gaps: Import references P_01260207233100000069 (a separate version of dir_id_handler) while the main dir_id_handler found is P_01999000042260125068

recommended_authority.reason: Central S10D-S12D pipeline implementation; imported by all key automation scripts

best_candidates.relevance_reason: DirIdManager: read, write (atomic temp+rename), validate .dir_id files. DirIdAnchor dataclass with JSON (de)serialization and validation. Also contains create_anchor factory.

conflicts_or_gaps: A second handler P_01260207233100000069_dir_id_handler.py is imported by dir_identity_resolver.py but was not found as a standalone file in the handlers directory - possibly inside idpkg_runtime or another location

recommended_authority.reason: Most complete implementation with full read/write/validate cycle

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Recursively scans for files needing IDs, allocates IDs via UnifiedIDAllocator (not facade), renames files. Interactive confirmation prompt.
risks: Bypasses IDAllocatorFacade by directly importing UnifiedIDAllocator - violates stated contract
No dry-run mode
No rollback capability

best_candidates.relevance_reason: FileIdentityResolver embedded in idpkg_runtime: parses filenames, detects ID presence/prefix type (python P_ vs plain), allocates via facade, renames file in-place during ingest_event.
Directory renaming engine: get_dir_rename_plan, validate_rename_plan, execute_rename with dry_run support.

conflicts_or_gaps: add_ids_recursive.py bypasses facade; FileIdentityResolver inside idpkg_runtime is the canonical path but is not independently importable

recommended_authority.reason: FileIdentityResolver inside IdpkgEngine is the current enforced path (uses facade, emits evidence, supports dry-run)

best_candidates.relevance_reason: JSON Schema (draft-07) for governance registry. Defines FileRecord (file_id pattern ^[0-9]{20}$, sha256, dir_id, governance_domain enum), EdgeRecord, and top-level files+edges arrays.
Schema for .dir_id anchor files used by DirIdManager.
risks: Duplicate: also present at ID/dir_id/.dir_id.schema.json

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Schema for the ingest envelope used during file/directory intake events.

conflicts_or_gaps: .dir_id.schema.json is duplicated in two locations (ID/3_schemas and ID/dir_id)

recommended_authority.reason: Top-level schema governing the REGISTRY_file.json SSOT; explicitly versioned v3

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Generates RFC-6902 JSON Patches (add/replace operations) from analysis results keyed by file_id. PatchGenerator class with generate_patches_from_analysis, find_file_by_id.
RegistryWriter._write_patch generates idpkg_patch_{event_id}.json files containing RFC-6902 operations as part of every upsert_file/upsert_directory call.

conflicts_or_gaps: Two independent patch generators exist: one in REGISTRY_AUTOMATION/scripts for column analysis, one inside idpkg_runtime for live ingest events

recommended_authority.reason: Live ingest patch generator integrated with the authoritative engine

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Applies RFC-6902 JSON Patches via jsonpatch library. Creates backup before application, writes evidence artifacts (pre/post SHA256, patch metadata). Supports dry_run.
risks: Uses shutil.copy2 backup (not atomic); if crash between backup and write, partial state possible
Requires jsonpatch library as external dependency

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Internal _apply_patch in RegistryWriter: custom RFC-6902 interpreter (add/replace/remove) without external dependency. Used by upsert_file and upsert_directory.
risks: Custom JSON Patch interpreter may not handle all RFC-6902 edge cases (e.g., move, copy, test operations not supported)

conflicts_or_gaps: Two independent applier implementations: external jsonpatch library vs custom interpreter in idpkg_runtime

recommended_authority.reason: More complete RFC-6902 support via jsonpatch library; supports backup and evidence

best_candidates.relevance_reason: RegistryWriter performs all registry mutations: load_registry (atomic_json_read), upsert_file, upsert_directory with patch generation and atomic_json_write. Enforces immutable_fields from write policy. This is the closest thing to a single-writer engine.
risks: No explicit single-writer lock at the engine level beyond atomic_json_write; concurrent IdpkgEngine instances could race

best_candidates.relevance_reason: PromotionPatch dataclass: CAS (Compare-And-Swap) via registry_hash precondition. Supports both simple changes dict and complex operation arrays. Includes normalization and policy validation flags.
risks: CAS precondition design not wired into any visible executor in the current repo

conflicts_or_gaps: No dedicated single-writer process/lock exists; mutation is guarded only by atomic_json_write file lock
PromotionPatch CAS design in src_modules does not appear connected to RegistryWriter in idpkg_runtime

recommended_authority.reason: Only active registry mutation path found in the current runtime

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Bidirectional reconciliation: REGISTRY_ORPHAN, DISK_UNREGISTERED, ID_MISMATCH, DUPLICATE_FILE_ID, DUPLICATE_PATH, MISSING_DIR_ID. Produces ReconciliationResult with severity-tagged issues.
Builds bidirectional sha256 → file_id and file_id → sha256 mappings, validates formats, generates SHA256_TO_FILE_ID.json for downstream use.

conflicts_or_gaps: Two reconcilers with different scopes: one does filesystem-registry bidirectional sync, the other is sha256/file_id mapping validation

recommended_authority.reason: Broader scope covering all reconciliation issue types; used by healthcheck

best_candidates.relevance_reason: GateRunner runs 14 named gates (X-G1 through X-G4, DIR-G1 through DIR-G5, FILE-G1 through FILE-G5) from GATE_CATALOG.json against every ingest envelope. Returns per-gate PASS/FAIL/SKIP results.
Pre-commit gate: enforces no imports of DEPRECATED/INTERNAL ID modules, no bespoke ID regex, no ad-hoc allocators, new ID scripts must be in ID_SCRIPT_INVENTORY.jsonl. AST-based.
risks: Requires ID_SCRIPT_INVENTORY.jsonl at repo root with schema_version 2.1.4; that file was found ONLY in backups, not in current repo root - gate will sys.exit(1) on every commit

conflicts_or_gaps: ID_SCRIPT_INVENTORY.jsonl required by IDCanonicalityGate exists only in backups directory, not in current repo root - this gate is broken in its current state

recommended_authority.reason: Live runtime gate runner integrated with ingest pipeline

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Comprehensive nightly health check: uses ScannerService, RegistryFilesystemReconciler, OrphanPurger. Produces HealthCheckReport with violation counts by severity (CRITICAL/ERROR/WARNING). Outputs evidence artifacts.
risks: Imports P_01999000042260125108_registry_fs_reconciler (different file_id than the 125106 version found) - possible stale import reference

conflicts_or_gaps: healthcheck.py imports P_01999000042260125108_registry_fs_reconciler but the reconciler found on disk is P_01999000042260125106 - import will fail unless 125108 exists elsewhere

recommended_authority.reason: Only dedicated healthcheck implementation found

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Primary ingest orchestrator. ingest_event handles FILE_CREATED, FILE_MODIFIED, FILE_MOVED, DIR_CREATED, DIR_MOVED, PROMOTE_REQUESTED events. Resolves identity, runs gates, writes registry, emits evidence. Fully integrated pipeline.
FileIntakeOrchestrator for the REGISTRY_AUTOMATION pipeline: calls Phase A/B/C analyzers via subprocess, collects JSON outputs, used by PipelineRunner.
risks: Calls analyzers via subprocess which may not exist (e.g., P_01260202173939000060_text_normalizer.py referenced but not confirmed to exist)

conflicts_or_gaps: Two different ingest orchestrators for different subsystems: IdpkgEngine for ID/dir governance, FileIntakeOrchestrator for column analysis pipeline

recommended_authority.reason: Authoritative ingest orchestrator for ID governance; fully integrated with gates and registry writer

best_candidates.relevance_reason: PipelineRunner orchestrates the full post-analysis pipeline: intake → Phase A transform → patch staging. Imports FileIntakeOrchestrator, PhaseATransformer, RunMetadataCollector, PatchGenerator. Has run_pipeline method.
risks: src/pipeline_runner.py is a second PipelineRunner with different design (envelope normalization focus); naming collision

best_candidates.relevance_reason: PipelineRunner focused on FCA-001 envelope contract enforcement: validate/normalize transformer outputs before staging. Different from REGISTRY_AUTOMATION version.
risks: Class name collision with REGISTRY_AUTOMATION version

conflicts_or_gaps: Two PipelineRunner classes with different purposes and no shared interface
No identified post-write trigger mechanism connecting IdpkgEngine writes to the REGISTRY_AUTOMATION pipeline

recommended_authority.reason: More complete implementation covering full Phase A-C pipeline

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Builds sha256→file_id and file_id→sha256 mappings from registry. Generates SHA256_TO_FILE_ID.json used as lookup cache by FileIDResolver.
FileIDResolver: resolves SHA256→file_id with cache lookup, authoritative source query, and optional generation. Implements FCA-005 fix. strict_mode fails closed on unresolvable IDs.
SHA256Backfiller: computes SHA256 for all registry files missing it, updates registry in-place with backup. Dry-run supported.
risks: No atomic write on registry update; crash between backup and write loses data

recommended_authority.reason: Generates the canonical SHA256_TO_FILE_ID.json lookup used by FileIDResolver

best_candidates.relevance_reason: Closest match: resolves SHA256 → file_id via cache + registry lookup. Not a general join resolver but serves the identity lookup role.
risks: This is an identity resolver, not a general-purpose lookup/join engine for registry queries

conflicts_or_gaps: No dedicated lookup_resolver or join_resolver file found anywhere in the codebase; no class named LookupResolver or JoinResolver exists

recommended_authority.reason: No canonical lookup/join resolver found; this component appears to be missing or embedded implicitly in other tools

best_candidates.relevance_reason: ColumnLoader: loads COLUMN_DICTIONARY.json at runtime, parses column definitions (name, type, nullable, default), caches, provides accessor methods. Used by EndToEndValidator and ColumnValidator.
risks: Hardcoded path to column dictionary two levels up from scripts/

best_candidates.relevance_reason: ColumnIntrospector: analyzes actual column usage in registry (presence, coverage %, type inference, sample values). Runtime introspection not schema-based.

conflicts_or_gaps: No single 'ColumnRuntimeEngine' class found; column runtime capability is split across ColumnLoader + ColumnValidator + ColumnIntrospector

recommended_authority.reason: Foundation of the column runtime; used by all other column-related scripts

artifact_groups.system_part: Any file that appears to be the canonical end-to-end orchestrator

best_candidates.relevance_reason: IdpkgEngine is the canonical end-to-end orchestrator: loads config+contracts, orchestrates IdpkgConfig, ZoneClassifier, DirIdManager, DirectoryIdentityResolver, FileIdentityResolver, GateRunner, RegistryWriter, EvidenceWriter. CLI commands: init, scan, watch, promote, verify. 1209 lines.
risks: WRITE_POLICY_IDPKG.yaml referenced but only UNIFIED_SSOT_REGISTRY_WRITE_POLICY.yaml was found in COLUMN_HEADERS; filename mismatch may cause load failure

best_candidates.relevance_reason: PipelineRunner is the end-to-end orchestrator for the REGISTRY_AUTOMATION remediation pipeline: intake → analyze → transform → patch → apply.

conflicts_or_gaps: Two independent end-to-end orchestrators: IdpkgEngine (ID/dir governance) and PipelineRunner (column analysis remediation) with no visible integration point between them

recommended_authority.reason: Comprehensive 1209-line orchestrator covering config loading, contract validation, zone classification, identity resolution, gate running, registry mutation, and evidence emission with full CLI

registry_shape_conflicts: REGISTRY_file.json (01999000042260124503) has schema_version 4.0 with files+edges+entities; governance_registry_schema.v3.json only defines files+edges (no entities array)
RegistryWriter._ensure_registry_shape initializes files, entries, entities - three top-level arrays; schema only requires files and edges
id_semantic_conflicts: P_01999000042260124489_id_scheme.py generates GEU IDs with prefix '99' incompatible with the standard 01260207201-prefix 20-digit schema
add_ids_recursive.py bypasses IDAllocatorFacade and directly calls UnifiedIDAllocator, violating stated contract
healthcheck.py imports P_01999000042260125108_registry_fs_reconciler but the file on disk is P_01999000042260125106 (different file_id)
duplicate_or_variant_files: REGISTRY_file.json has copies in archive/pre_cleanup_20260225_173348/ and archive/pre_prune_20260306_210353/ and AUTHORITATIVE_TOP_10_20260320/
stub_or_placeholder_files: P_01260207233100000008_watcher_daemon.py is effectively a stub: imports from repo_autoops module not present on disk, enqueues to WorkQueue with no visible consumer
missing_critical_components: ID_SCRIPT_INVENTORY.jsonl at repo root (required by IDCanonicalityGate for pre-commit enforcement; only found in backups/ID_docs_outdated_20260222/)
WRITE_POLICY_IDPKG.yaml (referenced by IdpkgContracts.load but only UNIFIED_SSOT_REGISTRY_WRITE_POLICY.yaml found in COLUMN_HEADERS - filename mismatch)
P_01260207233100000069_dir_id_handler.py (imported by dir_identity_resolver.py and idpkg_runtime.py but only 01999000042260125068 version found in handlers directory)
Dedicated lookup/join resolver (no LookupResolver or JoinResolver class found anywhere)
Dedicated column runtime engine (capability split across loader + validator + introspector with no unified runtime class)

biggest_blockers: ID_SCRIPT_INVENTORY.jsonl missing from repo root - IDCanonicalityGate (pre-commit gate) will sys.exit(1) on every commit, blocking all development
WRITE_POLICY_IDPKG.yaml filename mismatch causes IdpkgContracts.load to fail, which prevents IdpkgEngine from initializing
healthcheck.py has a broken import (P_01999000042260125108 vs P_01999000042260125106) preventing health checks from running
watcher_daemon.py depends on repo_autoops module not present - duplicate/conflicting watcher architecture with dir_id_watcher.py
Two independent end-to-end orchestrators (IdpkgEngine and PipelineRunner/REGISTRY_AUTOMATION) with no integration: ID governance and column analysis pipelines are siloed
